entries.description: Islamic Golden Age: Extraordinary advancements in mathematics, medicine, astronomy, optics, chemistry, and philosophy; preservation and translation of classical Greek, Roman, and Indian knowledge
entries.causation_evidence: House of Wisdom in Baghdad and scholars including Al-Khwarizmi, Ibn Sina, Al-Razi, Ibn al-Haytham

entries.description: Founding principles emphasizing charity (zakat), social welfare, protection of orphans, widows, and the poor
entries.causation_evidence: Quranic commandments and early Islamic governance in Medina

entries.description: Establishment of major universities and libraries (Al-Qarawiyyin, Al-Azhar, House of Wisdom) that became centers of global learning
entries.causation_evidence: Historical records of Islamic institutions of higher learning

entries.description: Strong religious emphasis on literacy, education, and the pursuit of knowledge as a duty
entries.causation_evidence: Quranic verses and Hadith encouraging seeking knowledge

entries.description: Inspiration for the founding of the International Red Cross through Henri Dunant's exposure to Islamic charitable traditions
entries.causation_evidence: Dunant's own writings referencing Christian and Islamic charity

entries.description: Global Islamic humanitarian organizations providing disaster relief, medical aid, and development assistance to millions annually
entries.causation_evidence: Islamic Relief, IHH, Qatar Charity, and similar NGOs

entries.description: Development of sophisticated legal, ethical, and philosophical traditions that influenced later Western thought
entries.causation_evidence: Works of Ibn Rushd, Al-Ghazali, Ibn Khaldun and others

entries.description: Application of hudud punishments (amputation, flogging, stoning, crucifixion, execution) in several countries under Sharia criminal law
entries.causation_evidence: Amnesty International, HRW, and UN reports on Iran, Saudi Arabia, Afghanistan, Brunei, etc.

entries.description: Severe persecution of religious minorities (Christians, Yazidis, Ahmadis, Shia, Hindus, atheists) in multiple Muslim-majority countries
entries.causation_evidence: USCIRF, Open Doors World Watch List, and UN reports 2024-2026

entries.description: Blasphemy and apostasy laws resulting in executions, death sentences, imprisonment, and mob violence in at least 13 countries
entries.causation_evidence: Pew Research, USCIRF, and Amnesty International documentation

entries.description: Global terrorism committed in the name of Islam by groups including ISIS, Al-Qaeda, Boko Haram, Taliban, Hamas, Hezbollah, and affiliates
entries.causation_evidence: Global Terrorism Database and reports covering 2014–2026

entries.description: Systemic gender discrimination under strict Sharia interpretations (guardianship laws, unequal inheritance, testimony, divorce, mobility, education, and dress codes)
entries.causation_evidence: World Economic Forum Gender Gap Report, HRW, and UN Women data

entries.description: Ongoing practice or tolerance of slavery and sexual slavery in parts of the Islamic world (Mauritania, Libya, Yemen, ISIS, Boko Haram)
entries.causation_evidence: Global Slavery Index, UN, and HRW reports

entries.description: Honor-based violence, forced marriage, and female genital mutilation in communities influenced by certain Islamic traditions
entries.causation_evidence: UN, WHO, and NGO reports on prevalence in Muslim-majority and diaspora communities

entries.description: Suppression of free speech, atheism, ex-Muslims, and criticism of Islam through blasphemy laws and social pressure
entries.causation_evidence: Pew Research and Freedom House reports

entries.description: Treatment of LGBTQ individuals under traditional Islamic jurisprudence in many Muslim-majority countries
entries.causation_evidence: ILGA World and HRW reports

entries.description: Ongoing armed conflicts and sectarian violence justified through religious interpretations (Yemen, Syria, Nigeria, Somalia, Afghanistan, etc.)
entries.causation_evidence: Uppsala Conflict Data Program and ACLED data

entries.description: Widespread use of child marriage in regions following certain Islamic legal traditions
entries.causation_evidence: UNICEF, Girls Not Brides, and Pew Research data

entries.description: Suppression of reformist, liberal, and progressive Islamic voices in many countries and communities
entries.causation_evidence: Reports on treatment of reformers and ex-Muslims

entries.description: Significant charitable giving (zakat) tradition that continues to fund humanitarian work worldwide
entries.causation_evidence: Modern zakat and waqf systems

entries.description: Architectural, artistic, and cultural achievements including mosques, calligraphy, and geometric art
entries.causation_evidence: Historical and contemporary Islamic art and architecture

entries.description: Emphasis on community, family cohesion, and moral discipline in many Muslim societies
entries.causation_evidence: Sociological studies on Muslim communities

entries.description: Rapid growth and global presence as one of the world's major religions with over 1.9 billion adherents
entries.causation_evidence: Pew Research religious demographic studies

entries.description: Contributions to modern medicine, mathematics, and science by contemporary Muslim scholars and institutions
entries.causation_evidence: Modern Islamic contributions in STEM fields
